structures.text: reading other people's AI discussions is useful because another person's knowledge changes the discussion path
readers can observe premises and reasoning moves they would not have elicited themselves
a question-sharing site should require conclusions
without a conclusion readers must infer the stance and may misread the discussion
public experiments can improve trust when transparent but reduce trust when failures are hidden
visible hypothesis method failures and results improve trust
organizational complexity makes government apps hard to use
security legal constraints fragmented responsibility and large-scale coordination complicate user flow mapping
structure reranking is not a replacement for vector search but a post-retrieval adjustment
vector search finds candidates and extracted structures adjust the order
a premise is a reason or supporting statement used to justify a conclusion
a conclusion is the claim that reasoning tries to establish
wrong structure extraction can make search worse by rewarding the wrong post
the extractor may invent a premise or assign the wrong structure type
positive feedback reinforces working directions while negative feedback identifies failure points
positive feedback reinforces a direction that already works
negative feedback identifies failure points and changes the system to reduce them
topical similarity is shared subject matter while argument similarity is shared reasoning path
argument similarity means using a similar premise-to-conclusion path
not all causal explanations should be boosted
causal snippets can pollute contrast or definition queries if query intent is ignored
a weak baseline can make an experiment misleading
a stronger vector baseline is needed before claiming value
after synthetic data passes use real-like exported discussion examples without secrets
keeping the same metrics makes the comparison stable
markdown bullets are not evidence of reasoning
formatting can make unrelated posts look similar
a search system should surface posts sharing a premise but reaching different conclusions
the same premise can support opposite conclusions
definition search needs posts that explain what a term means
definition search should not rank posts that merely repeat the term many times
